What it means: The champion's current attack target — the default aim.
A freshly re-acquired current target after a kill (Darius' recast).
The enemy with the least Health.
The farthest enemy on the board.
The farthest enemy (Lux's beam origin).
The farthest enemy within 2 hexes.
The farthest enemy within 3 hexes.
The nearest enemy.
The highest-Health enemy within 2 hexes (Jax's leap).
The enemy that was knocked up.
The enemy that just entered attack range (an On-Enemy-Enters-Range trigger's target).

Aim Target: Closest enemy behind Step 1 Aim target
What it means: The enemy directly behind an earlier step's target (a pierce's second victim).
The 4 nearest enemies (Count splits among them).
The N nearest enemies by trait tier (Kai'Sa's barrage splits among them).
The 4 farthest enemies — one shot each (Caitlyn).
The current target plus the 3 nearest enemies (Twisted Fate's cards split among them).
The current target and the enemy on each side of it (Karma's 3rd cast).
Every enemy carrying the Mark a previous step applied (Quinn's arrow rain).
The one enemy on each side of the caster (Sett's grab — up to 2).

Aim Target: Row or column with most enemies
What it means: The line (row or column) holding the most enemies.
A random enemy within 2 hexes of the current target, re-rolled per instance (Jinx).
The ally with the least Health.
The lowest-Health allies (Shen shields the 2 lowest).
The caster's own hex — a self-cast
column5: or the centre of a self-aimed AOE.

What it means: A hex away from enemies — a reposition/flee.
Whatever an earlier Step 1 aimed at (a sequenced follow-up / delayed blast rides it).
Whatever an earlier Step 2 aimed at.
The nearest enemy to a SUMMON's owner (the summon's own attack target).
The summoned unit itself acts (a second action source).
The densest cluster of enemies — the AOE centres on it (Jarvan / Sona / Thresh).

Aim Target: Aim Target is now a VALIDATED vocabulary (2026-07-18, the user's call): it names WHICH unit(s) an action targets, and every Hero cell must be a key here. It absorbed the target-SELECTION values that used to live in 'Spread' (Same target folded into the plain single-target aim; Each to its own target, Split across nearest/farthest N, Split across marked, Current + Left + Right became the 'enemy set' / 'per-instance' rows here). 'Spread' kept only geometric SHAPE and was renamed 'Volley Shape'.

'Kind' groups the values (single enemy / enemy set / single ally / ally set / self / position / per-instance / reference) — it is documentation, not a separate checked column. A multi-instance action at an 'enemy set' aim SPLITS its Count among that set; at a single aim it converges. '—' means no aim (a passive self-buff with Recipient Self, an untethered summon).
